channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: Lakers' Luka Doncic left stunned after getting ejected over interaction with fan
item.description: Luka Doncic was ejected in a tight game on Wednesday after he picked up a second technical foul for trash talking a fan sitting courtside against the Oklahoma City Thunder.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Timberwolves watch their playoff position tumble after blowing huge lead in 4th quarter
item.description: MILWAUKEE (AP) — Regular-season defeats don’t get much more brutal than the one the Minnesota Timberwolves suffered Tuesday. The wide-open nature of the Western Conference playoff race doesn’t give them much time to stew over what went wrong.
item.dc:creator: STEVE MEGARGEE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: After 2 years in an Argentine prison, former Masters champ Angel Cabrera is back at Augusta National
item.description: Angel Cabrera is back at Augusta National, walking the fairways at the Masters six years after his last appearance and less than two after he was paroled from an Argentine prison.
item.dc:creator: Dave Skretta, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: With top seed in East secured, Atkinson wants to get some of Cavaliers' top players some rest
item.description: CLEVELAND (AP) — Kenny Atkinson thought during training camp that realistic goals for the Cleveland Cavaliers would be 50 wins and a top-three seed in the Eastern Conference. The Cavaliers surpassed those goals awhile ago to the point where Atkinson is planning for a possible championship run.
item.dc:creator: JOE REEDY, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Jaylon Johnson wants to see real accountability before he says the Bears have changed for the better
item.description: Jaylon Johnson is heading into his sixth NFL season and third go-around riding Chicago's head coaching carousel, and is excited to see the positive change that has eluded them since he joined the franchise, and is looking forward to the Bears' fresh start with new head coach Ben Johnson.
item.dc:creator: Eli Ong, WGN 9

item.title: NHL first-round pick retires at age 21 due to tissue disorder diagnosis
item.description: Chaz Lucius, the 18th overall selection of the 2021 NHL Draft, was forced to retire at the age of 21 due to a tissue disorder diagnosis.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Grizzlies' Jaylen Wells breaks his wrist after hard landing sends rookie to the hospital
item.description: Memphis starting guard Jaylen Wells broke his right wrist on a hard fall on his arm and head from a midair collision at the end of a fast-break dunk during a victory over Charlotte.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Illinois school faces criminal DOJ referral after allegations of forcing girls to change with trans student
item.description: The law firm America First Legal (AFL) made criminal referral of the district Deerfield Public Schools 109 to the DOJ over allegations last month.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Texas' Arch Manning adds to growing NIL valuation, inks deal with clothing company
item.description: Arch Manning already holds one of the nation's highest collegiate athlete NIL valuations, and this week he increased that number by landing another brand deal.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Nuggets fire coach Malone and won't extend GM Booth in stunning move as postseason looms
item.description: Michael Malone coached the Denver Nuggets to the NBA title in 2023. He has them set to make the postseason for a seventh consecutive year. Even amid a four-game slide, they're still in position to have home-court advantage in Round 1.
item.dc:creator: TIM REYNOLDS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Astros' Spencer Arrighetti breaks thumb in freak batting practice accident
item.description: Houston Astros starting pitcher Spencer Arrighetti suffered an unfortunate injury in a freak batting practice accident on Monday prior to the team's game against the Mariners.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Walter Clayton Jr.'s defensive stop gives Florida its 3rd national title with 65-63 win over Houston
item.description: SAN ANTONIO (AP) — Florida’s Walter Clayton Jr. came up with the perfect going-away present for that spirit-crushing Houston defense that bullied, battered and bedeviled him all night. It was a defensive gem of his own. Right before the buzzer. For the win and the national title.
item.dc:creator: EDDIE PELLS, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: By The Numbers: How this might be the weakest bottom three in Premier League history
item.description: It seems like it’s never been harder for promoted teams to stay in the Premier League. It looks fairly certain that the three promoted teams will be relegated to the Championship after just one season for the second straight year.
item.dc:creator: STEVE DOUGLAS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Houston's Kelvin Sampson was close to his 1st national title. It slipped away painfully vs. Florida
item.description: SAN ANTONIO (AP) — All Kelvin Sampson could do was stand there, hands on hips with a blank look on his face, as the ball bounced loose and Houston’s latest chance at a national title bounced away, too.
item.dc:creator: AARON BEARD, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump honors Dodgers' 2024 World Series championship team
item.description: (KTLA) -- President Donald Trump welcomed Shohei Ohtani, Mookie Betts and the reigning champion Los Angeles Dodgers to the White House on Monday to congratulate them for winning the World Series last season. "That is a very good-looking group of people," Trump said as he began his remarks.
item.dc:creator: Marc Sternfield, KRON4

item.title: Justin Turner's error allows Fernando Tatis Jr. to score go-ahead run in Padres' 8-7 win over Cubs
item.description: Luis Arraez had four hits, Fernando Tatis Jr. scored the go-ahead run in the ninth inning when first baseman Justin Turner couldn't make the catch to finish a double play, and the San Diego Padres beat the Chicago Cubs 8-7 on Sunday.
item.dc:creator: SARAH TROTTO Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Women's NCAA Final Four tournament a 'slam dunk for Tampa's economy
item.description: Even though the women’s championship is over, Tampa businesses are calling this weekend a "slam dunk." This is the fourth time the city has hosted the NCAA women’s basketball Final Four, and it’s not just basketball fans who are winning.
item.dc:creator: Georgia McCarthur, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Diego Luna's brace leads RSL past Galaxy, 2-0
item.description: SANDY, Utah (ABC4 Sports) - Diego Luna scored two goals as Real Salt Lake cruised past the LA Galaxy Saturday afternoon at America First Field, 2-0. Luna scored the two goals in a five-minute span of the first half and Real Salt Lake (3-4-0, 9 points) controlled the rest of the contest.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Carmelo Anthony and Sue Bird highlight Basketball Hall of Fame class
item.description: Carmelo Anthony and Dwight Howard are going into the Basketball Hall of Fame later this year, not once but twice. And LeBron James and Chris Paul are part of the group that’s headed to the Hall as well, even before their playing careers end.
item.dc:creator: Tim Reynolds, Fox 5

item.title: Vucevic, White, Giddey lead the post-season bound Bulls past the Trail Blazers, 118-113
item.description: Nikola Vucevic and Coby White each had 31 points, Josh Giddey had a triple-double and the post-season bound Chicago Bulls beat the Portland Trail Blazers 118-113 on Friday night.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Capitals beat Blackhawks 5-3, Ovechkin scores two goals to tie Gretzky
item.description: Alex Ovechkin tied Wayne Gretzky’s NHL record by scoring the 893rd and 894th of his career, the second being the game winner, as the Washington Capitals rallied to beat the Chicago Blackhawks 5-3 on Friday night.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Doug Bruno 'feels great,' but says stepping aside is the right call as Jill Pizzotti takes over at DePaul
item.description: CHICAGO – Less than a week after Hall of Fame head coach Doug Bruno stepped down for health reasons, the DePaul women’s basketball program named his successor—and it’s a familiar name to fans.
item.dc:creator: Josh Frydman, WGN 9

item.title: Teaching Chicago's hard-working values, basketball legend Tom Kleinschmidt builds a dynasty at DePaul Prep
item.description: DePaul College Prep's boys' basketball team, coached by Tom Kleinschmidt, has won three consecutive IHSA championships, thanks to his hard work, discipline, and respect, which he learned growing up on the city's Northwest Side.
item.dc:creator: Mike Lowe, WGN 9

item.title: Baseball: Granite Hills 4, San Marcos 4
item.description: SAN MARCOS(KUSI)- Spring break baseball at the yard on Tuesday afternoon in the North County with this one ending in a 4-4 tie.
item.dc:creator: Nick James, Fox 5
